summary: Decompiled a large slice of the Stage Switch menu implementation in src/melee/mn/mnstagesw.c. The PR added typed per-GObj user data, menu text/cursor setup, two-column stage navigation, input handling, JObj animation/state updates, and GObj creation/teardown logic. decomp.dev reported +440 matched code bytes and +48 matched data bytes, with mnStageSw_80235DC8 becoming a 100% match and several other mnstagesw functions moving from 0% to substantial partial matches. There was no PR body or human review in the slice, so rationale is inferred from the diff and bot report.

key_files.role: Stage Switch menu decompilation and matching work

what_changed: Added sysdolphin/baselib/debug.h so the new allocation path can use HSD_ASSERTREPORT.
Introduced MnStageSwData with offset-style fields for menu state, six cached HSD_JObj pointers, padding, and an HSD_Text pointer array for all 29 stages.
Changed mnStageSw_802359C8 from a placeholder into text/icon creation for the two Stage Switch columns, using mnStageSw_stageIcons for unlocked stages and icon 0x25 for locked/unavailable stages.
Implemented mnStageSw_80235C58 as the helper that finds an available stage in the same 0-14 or 15-28 column, returning -1 when that column has no available stages.

Implemented mnStageSw_80235DC8 as D-pad navigation over the two-column stage list, including wraparound within each column and skipping unavailable stages; decomp.dev reported this function as 100% matched.

Implemented fn_80235F80 as the Stage Switch input proc: reads buttons, handles cancel/leave, handles toggling selected stages while avoiding disabling the last enabled stage, persists stage state, and routes D-pad movement through mnStageSw_80235DC8.
Added noinline-style wrapper helpers mnStageSw_80236178_noinline and mnStageSw_802364A0_noinline for calls into the existing icon positioning and JObj lookup helpers.

Implemented mnStageSw_80236548 to update hover/confirmation visuals using JObj child lookup, hidden flags, animation frame preservation, cursor position, and mnStageSw_803ED488 animation-loop settings.

Implemented fn_80236998 as the menu GObj update/state-machine proc, including transition animation handling, delayed text creation, per-frame animation advancement, text cleanup with HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4, and GObj destruction on exit.

Implemented mnStageSw_80236CBC to create the Stage Switch GObj, load MenMainConSs_Top, allocate/init MnStageSwData, cache child JObjs via lb_80011E24, load one MenMainCursorSs_Top cursor per stage, attach cursors to the two columns, hide locked cursors, and initialize hover position.
smart_moves: Grounded the decomp around a concrete MnStageSwData layout instead of continuing to treat user_data as raw bytes; this made the JObj and text lifetimes visible while preserving offset-based field names where semantics remain uncertain.
Kept original-looking two-column control flow with split ranges 0-14 and 15-28 rather than over-abstracting into rows/columns, which is important for matching this menu code.

Preserved matching artifacts explicitly: #pragma dont_inline on mnStageSw_8023593C, PAD_STACK(0x28) in fn_80235F80, u8 casts around stage indices, pointer increments, goto check_dpad, and small *_noinline wrappers.
Threaded stage availability checks through gm_801641CC/gm_80164430 consistently before drawing icons, showing cursors, accepting navigation targets, or writing enable state with gm_801641E4.
Mirrored UI state changes through changed_hovered and changed_confirmed flags so animation/JObj updates only run when the backing menu state changes.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT immediately after HSD_MemAlloc for the new user_data allocation, matching the project's HSD allocation/assert style.
decomp_lessons: For menu decompilation, reconstruct the GObj user_data layout early; child JObj caches and text arrays often explain later animation and cleanup code.
Two-column menu selection code may match better as explicit boundary cases and duplicated ranges than as a generic grid navigation helper.
Stage switch state has two related concepts: availability/unlock checked through gm_801641CC and gm_80164430, and enabled/disabled state read or written through gm_80164250 and gm_801641E4.
HSD_SisLib text objects need lifecycle handling in menu state machines: this PR creates 29 text entries after entrance animation completion and frees them on exit.

Cursor visuals combine multiple HSD_JObj operations: lb_80011E24 child lookup, JOBJ_HIDDEN flag manipulation, HSD_JObjReqAnimAll/HSD_JObjAnimAll, frame reads from mn_8022F298, and AnimLoopSettings tables.
When evidence is still partial, keep field names offset-based like x0, x1, x2, x1F, x20-x40 instead of inventing semantic names prematurely.
naming_conventions: Static functions and data keep the mnStageSw_ prefix plus address-style suffixes, e.g. mnStageSw_80235DC8 and mnStageSw_803ED4C4.
The new struct is semantically named MnStageSwData, but its members stay offset-based because the PR does not establish final field meanings.
NUM_STAGES names the 29-stage array length, while the column split remains expressed with literal bounds 15, 14, and 28 where needed for matching.
Existing asset/export names are preserved as externs: MenMainConSs_Top, MenMainCursorSs_Top, mnStageSw_803ED488, and mn_804D6BB5.
Wrapper helper names include _noinline, documenting their matching purpose without changing the underlying address-named helpers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline around mnStageSw_8023593C and retained a local pad[8] stack artifact.
Used PAD_STACK(0x28) in fn_80235F80 to preserve frame shape.
Added small trampoline wrappers mnStageSw_80236178_noinline and mnStageSw_802364A0_noinline around existing helper calls.
Kept pointer-walk loops for texts, icons, and stage IDs instead of purely indexing every access.
Used explicit do/while and goto check_dpad in the input handler, matching original control-flow shape.
Kept duplicated range setup in mnStageSw_80235C58 for low/high and start/end rather than simplifying the logic.
Relied on direct HSD_JObjGetTranslationX/Y calls and explicit f32 casts in position calculations, avoiding helper abstractions that could perturb codegen.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice, and the PR body was empty.
decomp.dev reported matched code 66.34% (+0.01%, +440 bytes) and matched data 38.71% (+0.00%, +48 bytes) for GALE01 c35dfb5..54b606b.
decomp.dev reported 2 new matches: mnStageSw_80235DC8 at +440 bytes from 0.00% to 100.00%, and main/melee/mn/mnstagesw .sdata2 at +26 bytes from 46.67% to 100.00%.

decomp.dev reported improvements for mnStageSw_80236CBC to 81.17%, mnStageSw_80236548 to 91.36%, fn_80236998 to 82.38%, mnStageSw_802359C8 to 87.54%, fn_80235F80 to 84.29%, mnStageSw_80235C58 to 75.85%, and .data to 72.46%.
decomp.dev also reported one unmatched-item regression: main/melee/mn/mnstagesw .sdata dropped by 4 bytes, from 76.47% to 61.90%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or rename fields in MnStageSwData.
Search for follow-up work that completes mnStageSw_80236CBC, mnStageSw_80236548, fn_80236998, mnStageSw_802359C8, fn_80235F80, or mnStageSw_80235C58 to 100%.
Look for later fixes to the main/melee/mn/mnstagesw .sdata regression reported by decomp.dev.
Compare the *_noinline wrapper tactic in this PR with noinline wrappers in other mn menu decomp files.
Search adjacent menu implementations using MenMainConSs_Top, MenMainCursorSs_Top, HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC, or lb_80011E24 for reusable menu setup patterns.
